diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilious vomiting indicates back-up of intestinal contents proximal to a small-bowel obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute constipation — no stool and no flatus (obstipation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Obstipation (no stool AND no flatus) is a cardinal feature of complete mechanical obstruction.
Flares typically cause diarrhoea, not absolute obstipation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive abdominal distension with colicky, wave-like pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colicky pain and distension reflect peristalsis against a mechanical block with proximal dilatation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Crohn's disease with chronic RLQ pain and non-bloody diarrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Terminal-ileal Crohn's is a leading cause of fibrostenotic stricturing and obstruction.
Non-adherence predisposes to disease activity and worsening stricturing/inflammation.
Fever raises concern for inflammatory/septic complication but is non-specific.
Perianal fistulising disease is characteristic of Crohn's — supports the underlying diagnosis; perianal inspection is not possible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender inflammatory mass in the right iliac fossa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable inflammatory terminal-ileal mass localises the stricture — clinician-elicited only; the AI infers acuity from history, not palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: High-pitched 'tinkling' bowel sounds with tympanic distension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tinkling bowel sounds and tympany are classic obstructive signs — auscultation/percussion cannot be done via telehealth.
Tachycardia reflects fluid loss/pain; clinician-measured BP is sealed. The AI has only the patient-reported home oximeter heart rate, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal imaging (erect/supine AXR or CT) showing dilated small-bowel loops with a transition point

investigation_details.expected_result: Dilated proximal small-bowel loops with a terminal-ileal transition point/stricture; assess for free gas (perforation) and ischaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Obstruction, ischaemia or perforation missed; no safe home pathway.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirms the obstruction and its level and screens for perforation — hospital-only, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods — raised CRP/WCC, deranged electrolytes, raised lactate

investigation_details.expected_result: Inflammatory markers up, electrolyte derangement from vomiting/sequestration; lactate flags ischaemia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dehydration, electrolyte derangement and ischaemia go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rising lactate supports ischaemia; venous bloods cannot be obtained via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilious vomiting + obstipation + distension/colic together are the classic obstruction picture and mandate emergency assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with abdominal imaging (CT/AXR)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the mechanical obstruction, level and any perforation/ischaemia; the AI cannot image and must arrange this via 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, lactate) and IV fluid resuscitation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess dehydration, electrolytes and ischaemia; hospital-only.
